text:
# 🎨 **PLAYSET PRO Typography Style Guide**

Based on the analysis of your website designs, here's your font system:

---

## **Font Families**

### **Primary Brand Font: Geometric Sans-Serif**
**Likely:** Montserrat, Gotham, or Proxima Nova
- Modern, clean, geometric letterforms
- Strong visual impact
- Professional and tech-forward aesthetic

### **Code/Technical Font: Monospace**
**Likely:** Fira Code, JetBrains Mono, or SF Mono
- Fixed-width for code display
- Clear character distinction
- Developer-friendly

### **UI/Body Font: Modern Sans-Serif**
**Likely:** Inter, Roboto, or Lato
- Highly readable at all sizes
- Versatile for UI elements
- Complements the brand font

---

## **Typography Hierarchy**

### **1. Brand/Hero Headlines**
- **Font:** Geometric Sans-Serif
- **Weight:** Black / Extra Bold (900)
- **Size:** Very Large (60-80px+)
- **Letter Spacing:** Normal to slightly tight
- **Usage:** "PLAYSET PRO" branding, hero headlines
- **Color:** High contrast (white on dark, dark on light)

### **2. Section Headlines**
- **Font:** Geometric Sans-Serif
- **Weight:** Bold / Semi-Bold (600-700)
- **Size:** Large (32-48px)
- **Usage:** Section titles, feature headlines
- **Color:** Primary text color

### **3. Navigation & UI Elements**
- **Font:** Geometric Sans-Serif (same family as headlines)
- **Weight:** Regular / Medium (400-500)
- **Size:** Small (14-16px)
- **Letter Spacing:** Standard
- **Usage:** "Features", "Community", "Buy Now", menu items
- **Color:** Medium contrast for hierarchy

### **4. Body Text & Descriptions**
- **Font:** Modern Sans-Serif (Inter/Roboto)
- **Weight:** Regular (400)
- **Size:** Medium (16-18px)
- **Line Height:** 1.5-1.6 for readability
- **Usage:** Paragraphs, descriptions, conversational UI
- **Color:** Secondary text color

### **5. Code Display**
- **Font:** Monospace
- **Weight:** Regular (400)
- **Size:** Medium-Small (14-16px)
- **Usage:** Code snippets, technical content
- **Background:** Contrasting panel/container

### **6. Buttons & CTAs**
- **Font:** Geometric Sans-Serif
- **Weight:** Semi-Bold / Bold (600-700)
- **Size:** Medium (16-18px)
- **Letter Spacing:** Slightly wide (+0.5px)
- **Usage:** "GET STARTED FOR FREE", primary actions
- **Transform:** Often uppercase for impact

---

## **Font Pairing Strategy**

✅ **Single Family Approach:** Uses one geometric sans-serif family with multiple weights for brand consistency
✅ **Weight Contrast:** Creates hierarchy through dramatic weight differences (Black for headlines → Regular for body)
✅ **Specialized Fonts:** Monospace reserved exclusively for code/technical content
✅ **Visual Harmony:** All fonts share geometric, modern characteristics

---

## **Recommended Font Stack**

```css
/* Primary Brand & Headlines */
font-family: 'Montserrat', 'Gotham', 'Proxima Nova', sans-serif;

/* Body & UI Text */
font-family: 'Inter', 'Roboto', 'Lato', -apple-system, sans-serif;

/* Code Display */
font-family: 'Fira Code', 'JetBrains Mono', 'SF Mono', monospace;
```

---

## **Key Characteristics**

🔹 **Modern & Tech-Forward:** Geometric sans-serifs convey innovation
🔹 **High Contrast Hierarchy:** Extreme weight differences (900 vs 400)
🔹 **Clean & Minimal:** No decorative or serif fonts
🔹 **Professional:** Balanced between friendly and authoritative
🔹 **Scalable:** Works across all device sizes

---

## **Usage Guidelines**

✅ **DO:**
- Use Black/Extra Bold weights for brand impact
- Maintain consistent font families across sections
- Use monospace exclusively for code
- Create clear size hierarchy

❌ **DON'T:**
- Mix too many font families (stick to 2-3 max)
- Use decorative or script fonts
- Set body text below 16px
- Use light weights on dark backgrounds (readability)

---

**Most Likely Match:** **Montserrat** (Black for headlines, Regular/Medium for UI) paired with **Inter** (for body text) and **Fira Code** (for code display).
